summary: Soundboard recording of a magical 1994 show with stellar Peggy‑O, So Many Roads and Terrapin jam, captured in good‑to‑excellent quality.
review: Fans repeatedly describe this performance as one of the best of ’94 – a scorching desert night that turned into a soaring second set highlighted by an unforgettable Peggy‑O solo, a soaring So Many Roads, a marathon Terrapin jam and a powerful Morning Dew.  The SBD source is generally praised for clarity, with only occasional complaints about a cut at the end of Dew or brief drop‑outs, so the recording remains very listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews are 4–5 stars and focus on strong musical moments; technical issues are minor, yielding a combined show‑plus‑recording rating in the high‑four range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most listeners note crisp, well‑balanced sound; occasional mentions of a brief cutoff on Morning Dew and isolated drop‑outs, but overall the mix is clean and enjoyable.

poor_songs: So Many Roads (vocal flubs noted by a few reviewers)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as uneven but solid; second set consistently praised for its energy and improvisational depth, with the heat of the desert giving way to a cooler, more focused late‑night performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for soaring guitar work on Peggy‑O, his emotive solo on So Many Roads, and overall presence; a few reviewers note occasional lyric flubs but consider him 'on top of his game'.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned for tight bass lines and the ‘Phil zone’ experience; praised for holding down the groove during long jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for a strong New Minglewood Blues performance and solid vocal contributions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards are noted as taking a back‑seat, which many fans view positively because it lets the other instruments shine; some still miss his more obtrusive style from earlier years.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill's drumming receives compliments for power and stamina (20‑minute drum segment) and for keeping the rhythm steady through long spaces.
